title: Consensus Topology
summary: A high-level lesson on thinking about participating nodes, shared coordination, and where confidential execution fits into network shape.

bodySections.title: Coordination Surface
bodySections.body:
Topology is about how components relate, not just how many of them exist. In confidential systems, that includes how work is split, where trust is minimized, and which participants are responsible for which pieces of computation.

A useful mental model is to ask what each node knows, what it does not know, and what it can still help verify.

bodySections.title: Execution Placement
bodySections.body:
Execution placement affects both performance and privacy. The more carefully the network distributes sensitive operations, the less any single participant needs to observe in full.

That distributed posture is what makes confidential execution feel like topology, not just cryptography.

quizQuestions.prompt: What does topology mean in the context of this lesson?
What question does the lesson suggest asking about each node?
choices: What it knows, what it does not know, and what it can still verify
